List<Integer> numbers = new ArrayList<>();

for (char c : expression.toCharArray()) {

The helper function is recursive. Let's define it as a private method in the Solution class.

private List<Integer> compute(List<Integer> nums, List<Character> ops) {
    if (ops.isEmpty()) {
        // base case: only one number, return it
        List<Integer> result = new ArrayList<>();
        result.add(nums.get(0));
        return result;
    }

    List<Integer> results = new ArrayList<>();

    for (int i = 0; i < ops.size(); i++) {
        // split into left and right
        List<Integer> leftNums = nums.subList(0, i+1);
        List<Character> leftOps = ops.subList(0, i);
        List<Integer> rightNums = nums.subList(i+1, nums.size());
        List<Character> rightOps = ops.subList(i+1, ops.size());

        List<Integer> leftResults = compute(leftNums, leftOps);
        List<Integer> rightResults = compute(rightNums, rightOps);

        char op = ops.get(i);
        for (int l : leftResults) {
            for (int r : rightResults) {
                int res = 0;
                switch (op) {
                    case '+':
                        res = l + r;
                        break;
                    case '-':
                        res = l - r;
                        break;
                    case '*':
                        res = l * r;
                        break;
                }
                results.add(res);
            }
        }
    }

    return results;
}

public List<Integer> diffWaysToCompute(String expression) {
    List<Integer> numbers = new ArrayList<>();
    List<Character> operators = new ArrayList<>();
    StringBuilder currentNumber = new StringBuilder();
    
    for (char c : expression.toCharArray()) {
        if (Character.isDigit(c)) {
            currentNumber.append(c);
        } else {
            numbers.add(Integer.parseInt(currentNumber.toString()));
            operators.add(c);
            currentNumber.setLength(0);
        }
    }
    numbers.add(Integer.parseInt(currentNumber.toString()));
    
    return compute(numbers, operators);
}

private List<Integer> compute(List<Integer> nums, List<Character> ops) {
    if (ops.isEmpty()) {
        List<Integer> result = new ArrayList<>();
        result.add(nums.get(0));
        return result;
    }
    
    List<Integer> results = new ArrayList<>();
    
    for (int i = 0; i < ops.size(); i++) {
        List<Integer> leftNums = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int j = 0; j <= i; j++) {
            leftNums.add(nums.get(j));
        }
        List<Character> leftOps = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) {
            leftOps.add(ops.get(j));
        }
        List<Integer> rightNums = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int j = i + 1; j < nums.size(); j++) {
            rightNums.add(nums.get(j));
        }
        List<Character> rightOps = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int j = i + 1; j < ops.size(); j++) {
            rightOps.add(ops.get(j));
        }
        
        List<Integer> leftResults = compute(leftNums, leftOps);
        List<Integer> rightResults = compute(rightNums, rightOps);
        
        char op = ops.get(i);
        for (int l : leftResults) {
            for (int r : rightResults) {
                int res;
                switch (op) {
                    case '+':
                        res = l + r;
                        break;
                    case '-':
                        res = l - r;
                        break;
                    case '*':
                        res = l * r;
                        break;
                    default:
                        res = 0; // should not happen
                }
                results.add(res);
            }
        }
    }
    
    return results;
}

solution: <algorithm solution in code>
```java
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;

class Solution {
    public List<Integer> diffWaysToCompute(String expression) {
        List<Integer> numbers = new ArrayList<>();
        List<Character> operators = new ArrayList<>();
        StringBuilder currentNumber = new StringBuilder();
        
        for (char c : expression.toCharArray()) {
            if (Character.isDigit(c)) {
                currentNumber.append(c);
            } else {
                numbers.add(Integer.parseInt(currentNumber.toString()));
                operators.add(c);
                currentNumber.setLength(0);
            }
        }
        numbers.add(Integer.parseInt(currentNumber.toString()));
        
        return compute(numbers, operators);
    }
    
    private List<Integer> compute(List<Integer> nums, List<Character> ops) {
        if (ops.isEmpty()) {
            List<Integer> result = new ArrayList<>();
            result.add(nums.get(0));
            return result;
        }
        
        List<Integer> results = new ArrayList<>();
        
        for (int i = 0; i < ops.size(); i++) {
            List<Integer> leftNums = new ArrayList<>();
            for (int j = 0; j <= i; j++) {
                leftNums.add(nums.get(j));
            }
            List<Character> leftOps = new ArrayList<>();
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) {
                leftOps.add(ops.get(j));
            }
            List<Integer> rightNums = new ArrayList<>();
            for (int j = i + 1; j < nums.size(); j++) {
                rightNums.add(nums.get(j));
            }
            List<Character> rightOps = new ArrayList<>();
            for (int j = i + 1; j < ops.size(); j++) {
                rightOps.add(ops.get(j));
            }
            
            List<Integer> leftResults = compute(leftNums, leftOps);
            List<Integer> rightResults = compute(rightNums, rightOps);
            
            char op = ops.get(i);
            for (int l : leftResults) {
                for (int r : rightResults) {
                    int res;
                    switch (op) {
                        case '+':
                            res = l + r;
                            break;
                        case '-':
                            res = l - r;
                            break;
                        case '*':
                            res = l * r;
                            break;
                        default:
                            res = 0; // should not happen
                    }
                    results.add(res);
                }
            }
        }
        
        return results;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n!)
space complexity: O(n^2)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: The solution uses a recursive approach to explore all possible ways to group numbers and operators, generating all possible results. It efficiently splits the problem into smaller subproblems and combines the results, ensuring all possible computations are considered. The use of recursion and combination of results ensures that every possible grouping is evaluated, providing the correct output for the given problem constraints.
